objects.name: Analytic 1654
objects.description: The defender observes a device at activation, supervision, or enrollment time with unusual management-plane posture, inventory, or trust characteristics and then relies primarily on downstream network effects and device state inconsistencies rather than direct low-level process telemetry. On iOS, the most reliable sequence is supervision/attestation or inventory concern near first contact followed by network egress or protected-state behavior that is inconsistent with lock state, setup phase, or expected managed app activity.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: iOS:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Supervised enrollment, activation, or inventory event reveals unexpected device property relationships, anomalous managed posture, unexplained configuration drift near first contact, or identity/inventory characteristics inconsistent with approved procurement baseline

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: VPN:MobileProxy
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Supervised or newly activated device initiates outbound connections to destinations outside Apple, MDM, update, or enterprise-managed baselines while locked, with no recent user interaction, or before expected app enrollment completion

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Managed app or device-originated network activity occurs while the device is locked or before expected managed app initialization sequence, inconsistent with expected background refresh baseline

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: iOS:unifiedlog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Supplemental anomaly in baseband, IOKit, accessory, security, or activation-related subsystem logging temporally adjacent to suspicious posture or network behavior

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Correlation window between enrollment/inventory concern and suspicious network activity.
Most strong posture and inventory analytics require supervised iOS devices.
Apple, MDM, update, enterprise, and managed SaaS destinations vary by organization.
Expected background network behavior varies by managed app set and policy.
Benign activation, restore, and setup traffic can be noisy immediately after provisioning.
Defines how recently the user must have interacted for activity to be considered expected.
Tuning for acceptable changes in inventory/configuration during upgrades or replacements.
